rationale: The cited paper is not just topically adjacent: it studies how specific language choices (explicit vs. implicit endorsement wording, including “I” formulations) change persuasion via inferred sender traits, which is conceptually close to the citing paper’s pronoun-based mechanism claims (empathy/agency and customer outcomes). Given this strong construct-level overlap and only a bibliography-level DOI match (no snippet proving it is incidental), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment rather than dropped.
